feed.title: SAP Community - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Extensibility
feed.subtitle: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Extensibility Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Triggering SAP_COM_0682 Service
entry.content:
Hello,

We want to trigger "Full Payment - Bulk Send" named outbound SOAP service from SAP_COM_0682 communication scenario.

I configured the communication arrangement, system, user. What we understood from limited informatiom on the API Hub, this service should be triggered automatically when FI-CA payment run happens or payment media file has downloaded. I did both but system didn't trigger the service.

Does somebody know how to trigger this service?

Kind regards,
Berke Günenç

entry.title: Released API DDLS Was Deleted ATC Error in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Despite Object No Longer
entry.content:
Hello,

I am working in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and I am facing compatibility ATC errors during ATC checks.

The following ATC errors are raised:

  • Released API DDLS ZDD_SD_TEST was deleted

  • Released API DDLS ZDD_SD_OPENORDERITEM was deleted

  • Released API DDLS ZDD_SD_DELIVERY was deleted

Error example:

Changes in ZSD (DEVC), compared to version 100 (CG4), found by component BC-SRV-APS-EXT-REL:
Released API DDLS ZDD_SD_TEST was deleted.

Background:

  • These CDS views were previously released as APIs (C1 contract).

  • The CDS views no longer exist in the system.

  • When searching in ADT (Ctrl+Shift+A), the objects cannot be found.

  • In the "Manage API Snapshots" application, I cannot find these CDS views in the current SAP Default snapshot.

  • However, the compatibility ATC check still reports them as deleted APIs.

I found SAP KBA 3442779 which explains that released APIs may remain in historical API snapshots and can continue to trigger compatibility checks even after deletion.

My questions are:

  1. How can I identify the exact snapshot that still contains these deleted CDS views?

  2. If the objects no longer exist and were never delivered to customers, what is the recommended way to remove these compatibility findings?

  3. Is setting the default snapshot to "Not Check Relevant" the only solution, or is there another recommended approach?

  4. Has anyone experienced a similar issue with deleted released CDS views in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition?

Any guidance would be appreciated.

Thank you.

entry.title: Analytical Query Inquiry
entry.content:
Hi SAP Team,

I have a concern regarding the result I am getting.

First, I joined PurchaseOrderItem (POForStoreOrderSummary) with MaterialDocumentItem2.



When I check the data using Customer Data Browser, I get the following results:

  • Goods Movement Type 643 = 10 quantity
  • Goods Movement Type 101 = 4 × 1 = 4 quantity

 

In the Analytical Query, I applied the following filters:

  • Goods Issue = 643
  • Goods Receipt = 101

However, I am having an issue with the result because the Goods Issue quantity is showing as 14.



Could you please help me understand why the Goods Issue quantity includes the quantity from Goods Movement Type 101?

Thank you.

entry.title: Fiori Generator error: "Edmx metadata is required" using Reentrance Ticket
entry.content:
Hi SAP Experts,

I am encountering an issue while trying to generate a SAP Fiori application (List Report Page) using the @SAP/fiori:app generator in VS Code.

Environment Details:

  • Target System: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

  • System Type Selected: ABAP Environment on SAP Business Technology Platform

  • Authentication Method: Use Reentrance Ticket

Steps to Reproduce:

  1. Open SAP Fiori generator in VS Code.

  2. Select "List Report Page" template.

  3. In Data Source step, choose "Connect to a System" -> "New System".

  4. Set System Type to "ABAP Environment on SAP Business Technology Platform" and Definition Source to "Use Reentrance Ticket".

  5. Enter the System URL and click Next.

Observed Behavior: As soon as I click the Next button, the Application Wizard immediately crashes and throws the following error message (as shown in the attached screenshot):

@sap/fiori:app generator failed - env.on(error) An error has occurred, please restart the generator. : Error: An error has occurred, please restart the generator. : Edmx metadata is required to continue with generation. Application generation exiting.

What I have checked:

  1. The browser popup didn't seem to block any authentication process, but the tool didn't route me further to fetch the service list.

  2. It seems the generator failed to fetch the Edmx metadata from the backend system via the Reentrance Ticket flow.

Could you please guide me on how to resolve this? Is there a specific communication scenario, catalog service permission, or Business Role missing for my business user in the S/4HANA Cloud system to allow metadata fetching via Reentrance Ticket?

Thank you in advance for your help!

entry.title: Revert the changes from Custom Field and logic app
entry.content:
Hi Team,

I need to revert the changes ACDOCA XREF1, XREF2, XREF3 from Custom Field and Logic. but when I am going to delete this, it is showing some error. I have attached the screenshot here.

entry.title: SAC to S4HANA .. Option to select ACCASTY field during Export job creation
entry.content:
Hello, 

We want to use field ACCASTY as the flag field to distinguish use cases we have from SAC to ACDOCP.

Therefore we want to be able to send it from SAC as it is possible like in Import Financial Plan Data app.

But unfortunately during export job creation in the pop up listed, there is no ACCASTY field. 

Question is which API or CDS view needs to be extended? We tried with the one CDS view A_FinPlanEntryItemVariableFix but it did not work.

Please support.

Thank you

entry.title: WMS API Equivalents in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition vs. SAP Business ByDesign
entry.content:
We are currently working with a customer who uses a .NET-based solution integrated with SAP Business ByDesign.

In the current ByD setup, warehouse operators work based on logistics tasks generated by the system (e.g., Goods Receipt, Stock Transfer, Picking). Our WMS consumes these tasks and allows operators to execute and confirm them. This process is supported through APIs such as SiteLogisticsTask and WarehouseConfirmation.

The customer is now evaluating a transition to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and would like to maintain the same operational model.

 

While we understand that S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition provides Warehouse Management capabilities and supports API-based integration with external systems, we are struggling to identify the equivalent of the ByD logistics task concept.

 

Our questions are:

  • Is there an equivalent to ByD Logistics Tasks / Warehouse Tasks in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition?
  • Can these tasks be created, retrieved, processed, and confirmed through standard APIs by an external WMS?
  • Which business objects and APIs should be considered as the equivalent of SiteLogisticsTask and WarehouseConfirmation in ByD?
  • Are there any limitations or recommended approaches when implementing a task-driven warehouse execution process with an external WMS in Public Cloud?

Our objective is to offer the customer the same warehouse execution experience they currently have in ByD while following SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition best practices and Clean Core principles.

Any guidance, architecture recommendations, or references to relevant APIs and business objects would be greatly appreciated.

entry.title: Need to throw an error message if the Text fields is not filled while saving the Quotation document.
entry.content: In S/4 HANA Public Cloud version 2601,need to throw an error message if the item level text fields is not filled while saving the Quotation document in VA21 tcode . Also, Text fields"From(DD.MM.YY)" and "To(DD.MM.YY)" must allow date format only, other values shouldn't be accepted.

entry.title: Unable to Map Place of Supply Field in Journal Entry Post SOAP API (SAP_COM_0002)
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

The "Place of Supply" field is not available in the Journal Entry – Post (Synchronous) SOAP API payload. We need to automate the posting process, and Create Outgoing Invoices is not the optimal solution. This gap is causing data inconsistency and impacting GST compliance in India. It is confirmed with SAP Support and this Influence Portal that the "Place of Supply" field is currently not available in API Journal Entry Post.

Customer Influence: Place of supply missing in Journal Entry - Post (Asynchronous)

Steps tried:

  • Reviewed API payload and I_JOURNALENTRYTP interface - field not found
  • Checked BAdIs - no relevant enhancement spot
  • Reviewed SAP API Hub docs - no reference to this field

Please let me know the workaround to include "Place of Supply" via API. Thank you so much.

entry.title: Not able to get CDS for tax rates based on tax jurisdiction
entry.content:
Dear all,

 

Further to SAP Note 3636847 – “Tax item information on Purchase Order Output Forms - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public”,

we are currently implementing Workaround 1 (i.e., implementing the BAdI to calculate values using custom logic).

However, for tax jurisdiction–based countries, we are unable to find a CDS view for the relevant tax codes rates based on the tax jurisdiction. The CDS view I_TAXCODERATE does not contain tax rates for such tax jurisdiction–based scenarios.

Could you please advise which CDS view should be used to retrieve tax code rates for tax jurisdiction–based countries?

Thanks in advance

Suddy

entry.title: How to edit Inspection Lot Short Text after Usage Decision (UD)
entry.content:
Hi Team,


We need a solution to allow users to update or enter the inspection lot short text after Usage Decision (UD) has been completed.

Currently, if a user enters incorrect text or misses this field, the field becomes read-only after the UD is made. This short text is mandatory for internal reporting and printing layouts.

Is there a standard SAP Fiori app or any other option that allows unlocking this short text field post-UD?

If no standard option exists, what is the SAP-recommended Cloud Badi, Developer Extensibility approach, API to safely update this short text on the inspection lot?

entry.title: Additional field in Manage G/L Account Master Data
entry.content:
Dear SAP Support,

We are working on harmonizing our chart of accounts in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and would like to maintain, at the G/L Account master data level, an additional field referencing the legacy account number used by our company prior to harmonization. We would therefore like to know whether it is possible to add a custom field to the application "Manage G/L Account Master Data" for this purpose, visible across all company codes (Chart of Accounts level).

Could you please confirm:

Whether such a customization (via the Custom Fields app or another means) is technically feasible for this application, and if so, which Business Context should be used.
Whether this service is chargeable.
Whether there is an existing standard field  that would meet this requirement, or any alternative standard solutions/future roadmap enhancements addressing it.

 

Thank you

entry.title: BAdI: Collection Basic Rule not getting customer to collection worklist
entry.content:
Hi SAP,

There is a user requirement to get all customers into Process collection worklist app regardless the customer has overdue or not yet due items. We implemented BAdI: Collection Basic Rule to test getting 1 customer into Process collection worklist but it does not work. Can you advise it the BADI support this requirement. Thank you.

Regards,

Leslie

entry.title: Output Parameter Determination: extend the condition parameters to include standard fields
entry.content:
Hi Anyone,

This specific example relates to the “Handling Unit” business rule. The goal is to be able to print material-specific HU labels. The material/product ID should be used for this purpose. Product: SAP Public Cloud!

The standard field “Material” is not available in the table column selection:
The result list contains 39 parameters but material is not available. Is there a way to extend the condition parameters to include standard fields?

 

What we’ve already tried—extending with a custom field:
We created a custom field named “YY1_MATERIAL” and copied the standard field using a BADI. Unfortunately, the only BADI is executed too late and only allows changes at the time the form is output.

Best regards

entry.title: Add Custom Fields in Look Up Retail Products app
entry.content:
I want to add custom fields under Information tab of Look Up Retail Products app.

 

I create custom fields in Custom Fields app with Business Context: Look up product information in retail stores (RFM_STORE_LKPPROD).

But from AdaptUI, custom field is not appear. Why?

Best Regards,

Khaing Zin

entry.title: Consumption of CBO created in Customizing tenant by ABAP / Dev extensibility created CDS view?
entry.content:
Can a Custom Business Object created in the Customizing tenant (key user extensibility) be consumed by an ABAP CDS view created in the Development tenant / Eclipse (developer extensibility)?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: How to hide "Post" button for general users in Create Supplier Invoice / Supplier Invoices List / MI
entry.content:
**Title:** How to hide "Post" button for general users in Create Supplier Invoice / Supplier Invoices List / MIRO?

**Body:**

We have a customer requirement to hide the "Post" button for general (non-approver) users in the following apps:
- Create Supplier Invoice (F0859)
- Supplier Invoices List (F1060A)
- MIRO

**Business background:**
The intended process is:
1. General user creates a supplier invoice and clicks "Save as Completed"
2. Approver reviews and approves via My Inbox

General users should never be able to post directly. We already have a BAdI implementation (MRM_CHECK_INVOICE_CLOUD) that blocks direct posting with an error message when the invoice status isn't "Parked and completed." However, the customer specifically wants the Post button to be **hidden**, not just blocked with an error.

**What we've tried so far:**

1. **Removing Business Catalogs** SAP_FIN_BC_AA_POST_REG_PC and SAP_FIN_BC_AA_DOC_PROC_PC from the role. This does hide the Post button, but other unrelated apps tied to the same catalogs also lose access as a side effect. We'd like to avoid this catalog-level approach due to that collateral impact.

2. **Using IAM App Authorization Variants** (available since 2502) to control access at a more granular level than the catalog. We found two variants tied to transaction F0859:
- F0859_TRAN ("Create Supplier Invoice") — description states this variant allows "processing and posting selection"
- F0859_77_TRAN ("Hold Supplier Invoice") — description states this variant allows "processing and holding (not posting)"

We deactivated F0859_TRAN while keeping F0859_77_TRAN active, expecting the Post button to disappear while Park/Save as Completed remained available. However, **the Post button is still displayed** after deactivation. Is this the wrong IAM App to target? Is there a different/additional app or restriction type that actually controls Post button visibility?

**Additional question on MIRO:**
Our environment also has the classic MIRO transaction. It doesn't appear when we search the IAM App list for "supplier invoice" apps tied to F0859. Does MIRO's Post authorization come from a completely separate business catalog/IAM app? If so, which one, and does it need to be addressed separately from the Fiori Create Supplier Invoice app?

**Environment:** SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Release 2508 (or later)

Any guidance on the correct approach (catalog restriction, IAM App Authorization Variant, or something else) — and confirmation on how MIRO fits into this — would be greatly appreciated.

entry.title: Can alternative reconciliation account and posting control (workflow / BAdI) be combined for supplie
entry.content:
Can alternative reconciliation account and posting control (workflow / BAdI) be combined for supplier invoices in Public Cloud?

**Body:**

I'd like to confirm my understanding of the following matrix for supplier invoice entry in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. Could someone with product knowledge confirm whether this is correct, or point out any mistakes?

| App | PO 3-way match | Alternative recon. account | Approval Workflow | Posting control (BAdI/standard) |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Create Supplier Invoice (F0859)** — MM | Yes | No | Yes | Yes (BAdI MRM_CHECK_INVOICE_CLOUD) |
| **Create Supplier Invoice - Advanced / MIRO** — MM | Yes | No | Yes | Yes (BAdI MRM_CHECK_INVOICE_CLOUD) |
| **Create Incoming Invoices (FB60)** — FI | No | Yes | No (not available in Public) | No |

My understanding behind each cell:
- Alternative reconciliation account can only be set in the FI app (FB60), because it is an FI function. In MM invoices (F0859 / MIRO), the reconciliation account field is greyed out.
- BAdI MRM_CHECK_INVOICE_CLOUD only applies to MM Invoice Verification (LIV), so it does not fire for FB60.
- The approval workflow (Flexible Workflow for Supplier Invoices) works with F0859 / MIRO but is not integrated with FB60 in Public Edition.

**My main question:**
Based on the above, it appears that in Public Edition there is **no standard way to use an alternative reconciliation account while also enforcing posting control via workflow or BAdI** — because the alternative reconciliation account requires FB60 (FI), but posting control (workflow / MRM BAdI) only works with MM invoices (F0859 / MIRO). Is this correct? Is there any standard method, configuration, or supported approach to achieve both at the same time?

**Environment:** SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Release 2508 (or later)

Any confirmation or correction based on documentation or project experience would be greatly appreciated.

entry.title: **Title:** Alternative reconciliation account works in Create Supplier Invoice (F0859) but fails in
entry.content:
**Title:** Alternative reconciliation account works in Create Supplier Invoice (F0859) but fails in MIRO (Msg F5439)

**Body:**

We are configuring the alternative reconciliation account function in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. It works in the Create Supplier Invoice (F0859) app, but fails in **MIRO (Create Supplier Invoice - Advanced)** in all cases. I'd appreciate help understanding why.

**Configuration we have done:**

1. **SSCUI 102631 (Define Alternative Reconciliation Accounts):** defined the alternative reconciliation accounts allowed for the standard reconciliation account.
2. **SSCUI 103508 (Define Account Group):** since the standard account group ABST cannot be modified, we created a new account group **ZZST** by copying ABST, and set the "reconciliation account ready for input" indicator as optional.
3. **G/L Account Master (Fiori ID F0731A):** assigned account group **ZZST** to both the reconciliation account G/L and the alternative reconciliation account G/L.

**Test results:**

| App | Invoice type | Result |
|---|---|---|
| Create Supplier Invoice (F0859) | G/L account item-based | OK |
| Create Supplier Invoice (F0859) | PO reference-based | OK |
| MIRO (Create Supplier Invoice - Advanced) | G/L account item-based | **Error (Msg F5439)** |
| MIRO (Create Supplier Invoice - Advanced) | PO reference-based | **Error (Msg F5439)** |

**Error message (F5439):**
"Mark the G/L reconciliation account as ready for input" — the message states that to enter a reconciliation account when posting a document, the account must be marked as 'reconciliation account ready for input' in the master, and that this condition is not met. The entered account is therefore not allowed.

**My question:**
With the exact same configuration, the alternative reconciliation account works correctly in F0859 (both G/L-based and PO-based invoices), but MIRO raises F5439 in every case. Why does MIRO behave differently from F0859 despite identical configuration? Is there an additional setting required specifically for MIRO, or does MIRO not support the alternative reconciliation account in Public Edition?

**Environment:** SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Release 2602

Any guidance based on documentation or project experience would be greatly appreciated.

entry.title: Change field selection in "Create Customer Project"
entry.content:
Dear Community user,

Background: In our project, there are hundreds of cost center in the system.
So, when our customer enter the Customer project or Internal project in the system, our customer wants to search Costcenter in the selection field.

Current Situation: The SAP Standard "Create Customer Project" does not provide search selection same as Project Manager or Customer field in the left side.
Also, When I put the costcenter code directly in the cost center field, the suggestion is not working.
Our customer is using Japanese language, english description search is working but Japanese description search is not working in current SAP verstion

Kindly share knowledge how to change this cost center field?

Best Regards,
